checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's structure and content accurately in the sections provided. Text extracted from images (such as the broadband dashboard on page 14 and the annexation form on page 32) is verified against the source visual data and is not fabricated. The metadata title is junk but is permitted under the 'Acceptable Changes' policy for AI-generated titles.
The URL present in the source (https://it.nc.gov/gicc) is correctly captured in the output (Reading Order 382). No fabricated URLs, emails, or phone numbers were detected.

The output suffers from significant silent data loss. While one block of sections is explicitly marked as 'omitted for token budget', there is a large gap between Reading Order 172 ('Accomplishments 2023-2024' heading from page 16) and Reading Order 286 (text from page 31). This jump skips over 14 pages of critical content, including major sections such as 'Value of GIS', 'Local Government Committee Initiatives', 'Statewide Efforts Based on GIS Data', and 'Collaboration for Data Governance and Consistency'.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains several major sections and detailed content between pages 16 and 31, including the 'Value of GIS' section (pages 17-21) and various 'Collaboration' sections (pages 26-30).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON skips directly from the heading of the Accomplishments section (Section 57, reading_order 172) to the Seamless Parcels text (Section 58, reading_order 286) without an omission marker, resulting in the loss of pages 16 through 30.

checks.a_analysis: The tables that are present in the output (Seamless Parcels, Annexation Form, Broadband Grants, Outreach) contain all the data rows visible in the source document. For example, the Seamless Parcels table correctly lists all 22 attribute rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Accomplishments 2023-2024' section (Section 57) is truncated immediately after the heading, losing the entire bulleted list of content that follows in the source on page 16. This is part of the larger gap identified in C3 but specifically constitutes a failure to preserve the content of that section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Accomplishments 2023-2024 (Page 16): 'Fiscal Year 2023-2024 was a productive year for the GICC. The following accomplishments are detailed through this section of the report: [List of 5 major bullets and numerous sub-bullets]'

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Wording in the provided sections matches the source document faithfully. No significant changes in meaning or data were found in the audited text and tables.

checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document flow correctly. No repeated text blocks or duplicated paragraphs were found in the provided JSON sections.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. While some major section titles are correctly identified as headings (e.g., Goal 1 at reading_order 157), others are incorrectly tagged as paragraphs. Specifically, 'Goal 2. Collaborate and conduct outreach...' (reading_order 167) is a major section title on page 16 of the PDF but is tagged as a paragraph in the JSON. This breaks the navigation structure for assistive technologies.
checks.b_evidence_source: Goal 2. Collaborate and conduct outreach for more integration of geospatial data in information technology for expanded benefits in the geospatial community in North Carolina.

checks.a_analysis: Table 117 (reading_order 117) is structurally corrupted. It attempts to convert a visual dashboard from page 14 of the PDF into a single table. This has resulted in the lateral merging of unrelated data points and labels. For example, the metric '$750M' is placed into a cell paired with the header 'Program Scope:', even though they are separate components in the source. This represents a failure to maintain the structural organization of the data.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual dashboard on page 14 containing separate boxes for 'Total Funds', 'Funds Awarded', etc., and a distinct data table below.
checks.b_evidence_output:
Row 1: ["Total Funds (Statewide)", "Program Scope:"]
Row 2: ["$750M", "Grant Award Details by County"]

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the document is logically preserved, following the flow from the cover page through to the appendices.

The nested list of accomplishments on page 17 of the source is converted into a flat list in the output (reading_order 22). The sub-bullets (e.g., 'Value of GIS StoryMap Project') are rendered as top-level items in the list array rather than using the 'nested_list' property to reflect their relationship to the parent item ('Value of GIS').
checks.b_evidence_source:
• Value of GIS
  o State Government GIS Users Committee Initiatives
    - Value of GIS StoryMap Project

checks.a_analysis: The conversion fails to preserve functional hyperlinks present in the source document. Active links such as the 'press release' on page 30 and the council website 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' on page 46 are rendered as plain text in the JSON without any associated URL metadata.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present but contains no link/URL property in the content span.
